Transcrição Completa: Day 29 - Sales Challenge Final Sprint

 

Conversa

 

[00:00]"Day number 29. You made it this far. Congratulations. You're almost to the pot of gold."

 

[00:06]"If you did not hit your 100 per day, I have really, really, really good news that I'm so excited to deliver for you."

 

[00:13]"Number one, I'm super disappointed in you for whatever excuses that you would possibly want to generate for why you did not hit your 100. You should also be extremely disappointed in yourself."

 

[00:25]"Number two, you get to go all the way back to day number one of the 30-day sales challenge because you are going to get audited at the end of your 30 days by me personally."

 

[00:36]"Because on day 30, I'm going to have you email whatever specific issues you had, whatever specific problems you had, whatever your uh, you know, excuses."

 

[00:45]"I'll be your therapist and I'll sit in the chair and I'll be happy, more than happy actually, to document whatever it is that in real time held you back and creating a problem for it."

 

[00:55]"That way we could have others overcome those very same issues in real time because the only thing that should be holding you back might be a mindset issue."

 

[01:04]"You need in real time to have a disciplined mindset in the sense that you're always going to hit your goals. You're never going to fall short."

 

[01:12]"You're going to go back and start over if you didn't do it the right way because if a job's worth doing, it's worth doing well. Okay?"

 

[01:20]"So in real time, if you want to build your business and you want to go through it and you want to hit all your other goals and fall short of them or whatever it is that you want to do and whatever standards you want to set, that's completely up to you."

 

[01:32]"Go ahead up here in the top corner in the search bar, type in the word duplicating and figure out exactly what you need to do in order to get your skills into somebody else."

 

[01:42]"That way in real time, you don't have to do it all. Somebody else can do it with you."

 

[01:47]"At which point, you have the ability to operate on that top level of your business in order to go out of your way to deliver, outsource, have the capacity to communicate with clients."

 

[01:57]"Get your project manager handling the operations, get your sales people handling your sales, go build your business, go position it, go do everything that's necessary in order to deliver."

 

[02:08]"Now, for all these people that you just closed, go through every single person. Don't forget about the people from day one, technically the hottest because you should have followed up with them a lot by now."

 

[02:20]"Also, go through your phone. Go through every channel in which you've communicate with people, LinkedIn, Messenger, whatever it is you're using. Email, every email that you're using."

 

[02:31]"Go through your phone list. Go through your salespeople's emails. Go through everybody's emails and get the hottest possible leads. Get your hotties right."

 

[02:40]"Get out a piece of paper, a whiteboard, whatever it is you want to use from left to right. Draw a piece of paper, straight line. This is your pipeline now from left to right."

 

[02:50]"Who's at the very beginning where they're initially contacted? Who's in the closing cycle? Who's in the in between where they're still in the working of the deal, right?"

 

[03:00]"And list out in real time where you have the most people at the beginning, in the middle, and at the end. And at the very end, those are your hotties."

 

[03:09]"This is day number 29. In real time, you need to go follow up with all the hotties. You need to follow up with all the sub hotties. Then you follow up with all the lukewarms."

 

[03:19]"You need to follow up with everybody that's kind of cold that you need to warm up a little bit. Like your food came out kind of warm, but you got to put it back in the microwave."

 

[03:28]"And then you got to go out once again and create new communication cycles because all this is a big flow, right? All of it in real time. It's just a bunch of cycles that you have to close."

 

[03:39]"And all of it should never stop. Technically, you shouldn't be focused on it like this. All of it should be moving at one time. It's a giant system."

 

[03:48]"It's not just you taking people from here to here. It's you taking people from here to here to here to here to here to here to here to here and everywhere in between at the same exact time while the whole system is doing its thing."

 

[04:02]"It's a whole shebang you got to build here. It's a business we're talking about. This isn't just some side hustle where you're calling people trying to be a scam and take their money."

 

[04:12]"You're not Bernie Madoff. You're an agency owner and you're a DMM student. And I'm excited to see that you got all the way to day 29. You got one day left."

 

[04:22]"Go out, hit your numbers. I'm glad you got this far. I'm proud of you if you've been hitting your numbers. And I'm proud of you if you made up for that fact."

 

[04:31]"But if not, get your ass back to day one and take it all over again. You got another 30 days in front of you."

 

[04:38]"If you still didn't get your sales right, go back, do it again. You got to get better. You got to build your skills. There's no bitching. There's no complaining."

 

[04:46]"If you didn't do a good job, you didn't do a good job. I didn't type your emails up. You typed your emails up."

 

[04:52]"You have access to every single resource. You have access to the private Facebook community where you can get peer-to-peer support on exactly what's working."

 

[05:01]"There's literally not one single excuse for somebody who's on pace once again to be a seven figure digital agency owner looking to sell this thing in the next few years."

 

[05:11]"I'm going to teach you how to do that, too. By the way, in real time, you're on day 29. You're trying to build your business."

 

[05:18]"You're trying to duplicate yourself to sales people. You're trying to deliver, do all this at once, but you have to hit your 100 because it's day 29."

 

[05:28]"See you tomorrow."

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

Dia 29 de 30 do desafio de vendas

Meta: 100 contatos por dia

Objetivo final: agência digital de 7 figuras

1 dia restante no desafio

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Pipeline de Vendas: Sistema de categorização de leads (início → meio → fechamento)

Categorização de Leads:

Hotties (mais quentes - prontos para fechar)

Sub-hotties (quase prontos)

Lukewarms (mornos - precisam aquecimento)

Cold (frios - precisam reativação)

Multi-canal Follow-up: LinkedIn, Messenger, Email, Telefone

Sistema de Duplicação: Buscar "duplicating" para replicar habilidades em outros

Estrutura de Time: Project Manager (operações) + Sales People (vendas)

 

ESTRUTURA:

Auditoria Pessoal: Email no dia 30 documentando problemas/desculpas

Accountability: Se não atingiu meta, voltar ao dia 1

Pipeline Visual: Linha da esquerda para direita mapeando jornada do cliente

Sistema Cíclico: Fluxo contínuo e simultâneo em todas as etapas

Hierarquia Operacional: Founder no topo → PM → Sales → Delivery

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Meta de agência de 7 figuras ($1,000,000+)

Planejamento de exit strategy (venda da agência nos próximos anos)

 

MINDSET E FILOSOFIA:

"If a job's worth doing, it's worth doing well"

Zero tolerância para desculpas

Disciplina absoluta para atingir metas

Mentalidade de dono de negócio vs side hustle

Responsabilidade total pelos resultados

 

RECURSOS DISPONÍVEIS:

Comunidade privada do Facebook para suporte peer-to-peer

Acesso a todos os recursos do programa DMM

Sistema de duplicação de habilidades

Auditoria pessoal no dia 30

 

DURAÇÃO: ~5:30 minutos FORMATO: Vídeo motivacional/instrucional direto para câmera - Dia 29 do Desafio de Vendas de 30 Dias
